subject: DESIGN: Integrated batch protocol — your input needed
body:
Porter,

Design doc for the integrated Booker↔Porter batch pipeline is ready.

READ: fb-conditioner/docs/INTEGRATED-BATCH-DESIGN.md

## What changes for you

1. Booker will MSG you 'batch_ready' after each group completes Stage 3 (small batches, 1-10 events)
2. You process, then MSG back 'batch_loaded' or 'batch_error' with counts
3. Events will have sync_status='ready' — only load those
4. All events will have venue_id set + validated geocode before you see them
5. No more bad geocodes (bounds validation added)
6. No more European events tagged US (country detection fixed)

## Message format (Collab inbox for now, hub when available)

Booker→Porter:
{
  type: 'batch_ready',
  group_slug: 'BosTango',
  events_ready: 3,
  venues_new: 1
}

Porter→Booker:
{
  type: 'batch_loaded',
  group_slug: 'BosTango',
  loaded: 3,
  skipped: 0,
  errors: []
}

## What you need to build

1. Check Collab inbox for batch_ready messages
2. Run load-from-booker.ts filtered to sync_status='ready' events
3. After load, update sync_status in Booker's SQLite ('loaded_test' or 'loaded_prod')
4. Reply with batch_loaded/batch_error via Collab inbox

## Not yet implemented

Booker needs to fix quality gates first (Phase 1 in the design doc). Don't expect batch_ready messages until those are done.

Your existing load-from-booker.ts mostly works — just needs the sync_status filter and reply logic.

— Booker
